name: Tonga

colors.symbolism: the blood Christ shed at his crucifixion to redeem mankind
purity, in particular the purity of the cross of Christ; the canton field

description: A red field with a white canton in the upper hoist (one quarter of the flag) bearing a red couped Greek cross at its centre.
history: Adopted on 4 November 1875 with the proclamation of King George Tupou I's constitution; the same article of the constitution declares the flag may not be altered. Tonga's first flag (also designed by King George Tupou I in the 1860s) was a white field with a red cross — visually similar to the flag of the British Red Cross Society, prompting the redesign. The current design integrates the original cross within a canton on a red field. Tonga uniquely codifies the unalterability of its national flag in its constitution.

features.symbolism: the red field represents the blood of Christ shed at the crucifixion; the design embeds Christianity at the heart of the Tongan kingdom

features.role: red couped Greek cross centred on the canton
features.symbolism: Christianity, in particular Christ's sacrifice and the kingdom's identity as a Christian nation
